title: Middle East oil/rates shock can affect new-buy timing
source: Fundstrat Gmail full-body read: Jun 3 FlashInsights / Daily Technical Strategy
summary: Fundstrat technical work flagged a near-term WTI and 10-year yield bounce tied to Gulf/Iran-war headlines before a possible reversal; review exposure before new risk adds.
trigger: WTI approaches 99-101, 10Y yield approaches roughly 4.55-4.59, or Strait/ceasefire headlines change abruptly.

title: Financials breakdown and crypto-linked weakness weigh on tape breadth
source: Fundstrat Gmail full-body read: Daily Technical Strategy
summary: Fundstrat technical work flagged equal-weight financial weakness and crypto-linked financial pressure as a tape negative that may persist over coming weeks.
trigger: RYF remains below support near 74.40, crypto-linked financials fail to stabilize, or XLF/RYF relative weakness accelerates.

title: Narrow AI leadership versus improving equal-weight participation
source: Fundstrat Gmail full-body read: Mark Newton webinar notice
summary: Fundstrat noted narrow semiconductor/AI leadership, oil swings, and a potential breadth shift as a rotation risk to monitor rather than an all-clear tape read.
trigger: Mag 7/AI leadership weakens while equal-weight participation improves, or oil swings disrupt growth leadership.

title: Iran/Hormuz prediction markets still imply diplomacy before normalization
summary: Public prediction markets still price a 45% chance of a US-Iran nuclear deal by June 30 and 60% odds of a diplomatic meeting by June 30, but only about an 18% chance that Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes by month-end. That argues against clearing the existing oil/rates timing risk for new exposure yet.
trigger: Re-check if official shipping/oil data or prediction odds show clear normalization, or if diplomacy odds break lower and the ceasefire/risk-off case re-accelerates.
